digest.component_or_area: op-cert merge-gate CI verification lane (mirroring verify-identity gate)
propositions: Add a required-environment op-cert verification lane as a step in the existing verify-identity merge-gate, reusing the merged-log walk and config-driven posture/boundary rather than a parallel walker.

The new rebar verify-opcert CLI walks the merged log, groups events by ticket_id, and verifies each in-scope ticket's completion-verifier op-cert, exiting 1 only when an enforced ticket lacks a valid required-environment op-cert.
Posture and grandfather boundary are read from rebar.toml (verify.require_environment, verify.opcert_enforce_since) mirroring identity.*, keeping the lane advisory until producers exist.

For a ticket lacking an op-cert, the enforcement anchor is the commit introducing its terminal close STATUS event, resolved via authorship.resolve_event_commit and tested against verify.opcert_enforce_since.
Fixture tests prove the gate: a valid op-cert passes while missing or foreign-key-signed op-certs fail, and a CI-trigger audit comment enumerates and classifies every workflow trigger.
The story is blocked on keystone op-cert storage/producer primitive requiring a reducer change to store DSSE envelopes on tickets.
